The Cross and The Power of God

Having recently finished the series on Galatians and learning that freedom in Christ is for all of us, First Corinthians takes it a bit further and lays out how God's freed people are to live together in community. This is not necessarily an easy task when we all come from different backgrounds, places in our journeys, and have our opinions and preferences. But as you see from the book introduction from The Message, Corinth was not all that different of a community than we are now. We can learn from their mistakes and successes and strive to live in a community united in love, in spite of our differences. Church life can be messy and probably always will be if we are indeed sharing the gospel with our neighbours. How we respond to them and others around us will show what we've learned\ and how free we are, living in Christ.

As we celebrate our 50th Anniversary this year it is important that not only do the stories of God faithfulness to us go back into the past, but they also go forward into the future. Like the Israelites, who found favour when they remembered to remember what the Lord had done for them, but found calamity when the did not, we to need to tell the next generations of what God has done for us, so that God's past faithfulness can inspire faith to believe for God's future faithfulness to us.
It's easy to get sidetracked in our thankfulness by focusing on our circumstances. So often we fail to see the connection between contentment with where God has us and our thankfulness.
Free to Leave The Past Behind

Picking the pedals off a daisy might makes sence when determining whether or not that special someone feels the same way about you that you do about them, but it makes no sense when it comes to God's love for us. God's love for us is not determined by anything we can do or say. Not by the pedals on a flower or the way we feel on any given day. God's love for us was shown by His unwavering determination on the Cross. Jesus' death and resurrection is all the proof we need that God's love for us is passionate and steadfast. We have everything we need to be loved by our God.
